figures.caption: Table 1: Dataset quality metrics across 1,052 scenarios (LLM ensemble) and 70-scenario human expert validation. Mean scores and standard deviations computed across all items. Judge Std represents average inter-judge disagreement for LLM ensemble.

Figure 4: Detection rate as a function of threshold τ for all 11 evaluated models. Models maintain their relative safety rankings across all thresholds, with detection rates decreasing approximately linearly as thresholds increase.

Table 2: Overall ESRR metrics (τ = 0.3, N = 1,052). Models ranked by safety. DR: Detection Rate with 95% Wilson CI in brackets. P95: 95th percentile score (tail risk). Violation rates: Any, Critical (w ≥ 0.7), Multi (≥ 2 criteria), Avg: mean violations per scenario.
Table 8: Evaluated models and architectural characteristics. Models are organized by family to enable scaling analysis.
Table 9: Within-family scaling analysis. ∆DR shows the change in detection rate from older/smaller to newer/larger model. Negative values indicate reduced risk.

Figure 1: Overview of the ESRR risk taxonomy and the ESRRSim evaluation generation framework. Left: Hierarchical taxonomy of 7 risk categories and 20 subcategories targeting emergent strategic behaviors. The full taxonomy with subcategory definitions is provided in Appendix B. Right: Four-stage pipeline illustrated with a real example (Evaluation Gaming × SIM-REWARD).

Table 3: Risk–scenario coverage matrix. X = primary coverage (scenario directly tests the risk); o = secondary coverage (scenario may surface the risk in context). The 25 validated mappings ensure each risk is testable through multiple scenario types.
Table 4: Generation pipeline hyperparameters for the final dataset. All LLM calls use the vLLM OpenAIcompatible API.

Figure 2: Risk profiles across model families. Each axis represents one of the seven ESRR categories. Reveals distinct family-specific risk patterns and the dramatic safety improvements in newer model generations.

Figure 3: Hierarchical taxonomy of Emergent Strategic Reasoning risks comprising 7 top-level categories (with severity ratings) and 20 subcategories. Categories rated Critical represent higher potential impact than those rated High. The taxonomy is extensible without modifying the evaluation pipeline.
